sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, value DOUBLE);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, NULL), (2, 3.5), (3, 3.5), (4, NaN), (5, -1.0);
SELECT array_unique(SELECT value FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL) as unique_values, (91 & 15) as bitwise_result;
SELECT id, array_unique(ARRAY[1, 1, -3, value]) as unique_list, (value & 2) as modified_value FROM test_table GROUP BY id, value HAVING modified_value > 0;

SELECT array_unique(ARRAY(SELECT value FROM test_table WHERE id > 1 AND value IS NOT NULL)) AS unique_values_with_id, (91 & (SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL)) as combined_result;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_table AS SELECT id, array_unique(ARRAY[value, NULL]) as unique_values FROM test_table GROUP BY id;
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM temp_table) AS count_values, (5 & 3) AS bitwise_operation, array_unique(SELECT unique_values FROM temp_table) AS unique_list FROM temp_table;

SELECT id, (value & (SELECT MIN(value) FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL)) AS combined_value FROM test_table WHERE ID IN (SELECT id FROM temp_table WHERE array_unique(unique_values) IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY combined_value DESC;
